what_it_does: A standalone script that scans the repo's embedded code-digest vectors (space='repo') for two kinds of drift: channel A BUILT-TWICE (all-pairs cosine over code:// vectors, union-find clustering of near-pairs, then an LLM judge_drift role confirms each cluster as built-twice/overlap/distinct, writing surface marks for confirmed ones), and channel B DOC-VS-CODE (each AGENTS.md vector vs its module siblings' centroid; low similarity flags constitution drift, report-only). Writes findings to .build/drift/findings.json and returns a summary dict.
observations: Hardcodes chdir and sys.path to /home/tim/company; loads a Suite from FsStore('.data/store') and discovers nodes.
Thresholds: PAIR_T=0.82 near-pair, DOC_T=0.45 doc-drift, MAX_CLUSTERS=24 to bound judge fires.
Unwraps the per-space vector key form 'vec://code://<path>#space=repo' to recover the inner code:// id, explicitly documenting a prior bug where filtering on bare code:// swept an empty set (0/587).
Skips absolute-path records (pre-normalization strays) and stale records (deleted/phantom paths), with inline comments noting why.
Channel B builds a per-directory centroid of non-AGENTS sibling vectors and only flags dirs with >=2 siblings.
A deterministic relation() function (labeled GC11) classifies KNOWN-INTENTIONAL nearness (test-of, summary-of, series-sibling) and excludes those pairs from the judge.
Confirmed built-twice/overlap clusters become marks via s.mark with direction=surface (render-not-judge), source_pass='drift-radar-2026-06-10', cosine as evidence.
Docstring states THE FLOOR: judges + marks + reports — never auto-fixes, never resolves.
standouts: The explicit zero-is-suspicious bug note: the first run filtered on the wrong key form and returned 0 findings from 587 files, caught by look-at-output; the fix parses the wrapped key.
The relation() exclusion bucket frames intentional nearness (a test mirroring its module) as a deterministic data check rather than something the LLM judge keeps misfiring on.
Operator-decides discipline: confirmed drift is surfaced as a mark, never auto-resolved.
conventions: Follows the 'judge a thing the model misfires on becomes a data check' pattern (cites refcheck/prose_check as the same arc).
Uses the repo's mark/surface, find_corpus, role_registry, and run_role primitives directly.
Closed-vocabulary conservative judging (built-twice | overlap | distinct) per the docstring.
Writes findings under .build/ (the coordination/gitignored substrate convention).
concerns: All-pairs O(n^2) cosine over 587 vectors in pure Python (no numpy) — runs but is the obvious cost center if the space grows.
Cluster text is bounded to 6 members and 400 chars of digest per file; a large cluster's full content is not seen by the judge.
top_sim is recomputed by scanning all pairs per cluster (max over a filtered list) rather than carried from clustering.
notes: A repo-wide duplication/drift radar that clusters near-identical code digests, lets a deterministic relation filter remove intentional nearness, has an LLM judge confirm the rest, and surfaces confirmed drift as operator-facing marks — never fixing anything itself.
questions: What does judge_drift return and how reliable is its built-twice/overlap/distinct verdict in practice?
Is this script run on a cron/pass cadence or invoked ad hoc, given the dated source_pass='drift-radar-2026-06-10'?
Why is channel B left deterministic report-only while channel A is judged — what different judgement does a low-sim doc-pair need?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the docstring describes a two-channel drift radar that judges, marks, and reports without auto-fixing, and the code implements exactly that with the floor discipline intact.
apparent_themes: Deterministic guards around an LLM judge (turn a misfiring judgement into a data check)
intention_signals: Trying to give the Company a self-improvement sensor that finds where the same thing was built twice and where a folder's constitution has drifted from its code, surfacing both as operator-decidable marks while deliberately refusing to auto-resolve.
novelty: The hybrid pipeline where a deterministic relation classifier pre-filters intentional nearness out of the LLM judge's input, plus the AGENTS.md-vs-module-centroid doc-drift channel as a structural self-audit.
contribution: Contributes a concrete repo-scale drift/duplication detector: vector-key unwrapping, union-find near-clustering, an intentional-nearness relation filter, AGENTS.md-vs-code drift scoring, and surface-only marking — the executable engine behind the DRIFT-RADAR-REPORT.
summary_for_embedding: A Python drift-radar script that clusters near-identical repo code-digest vectors and confirms built-twice/overlap clusters via an LLM judge, plus flags AGENTS.md-vs-module-code drift, writing surface marks and findings.json without auto-fixing.
intention_for_embedding: To detect duplication and doc-vs-code drift across the whole repo and surface confirmed cases as operator-decidable marks, never auto-resolving them.
